channel.title: Debt Help Form Blog
channel.description: Plain-language debt relief guides from Debt Help Form — consolidation, settlement, credit and hardship programs, and your consumer rights.

item.title: What the FDCPA Means for Everyday Borrowers
item.description: The Fair Debt Collection Practices Act sets ground rules for how third-party collectors may contact you, and understanding it helps you respond calmly.

item.title: What to Do When a Debt Is Not Yours
item.description: If a collector pursues a debt you do not recognize, you have the right to dispute it and to respond if identity theft is involved.

item.title: Understanding the Statute of Limitations on Debt
item.description: Old debts can become time-barred, and knowing how the statute of limitations works helps you avoid accidentally restarting the clock.

item.title: How to Build a Starter Emergency Fund While in Debt
item.description: Learn how a small emergency buffer can help you avoid new debt while you work through repayment, with practical steps you can start today.

item.title: The 50/30/20 Budget Explained for Debt Payoff
item.description: Understand how the 50/30/20 budgeting framework works and how to adapt its percentages to accelerate debt payoff without overcomplicating your finances.

item.title: How to Track Spending Without a Fancy App
item.description: Discover simple pen-and-paper and spreadsheet methods to track your spending accurately, build awareness, and free up money for debt payments.

item.title: How to Cut Monthly Bills to Free Up Debt Payments
item.description: Learn practical ways to negotiate bills, trim subscriptions, and lower recurring costs so you can redirect more money toward paying off debt.

item.title: How to Prioritize Which Debt to Pay First
item.description: Compare the avalanche and snowball methods and learn how to decide which debt to prioritize based on interest, balances, and your own motivation.

item.title: Protecting Your Personal Data When Seeking Debt Help
item.description: Practical steps to guard your personal and financial information from phishing and misuse while researching and pursuing debt help.

item.title: How to Read Your Credit Report Line by Line
item.description: Learn how to read every section of your credit report line by line so you can spot errors, understand accounts, and track your financial health.

item.title: How to Dispute an Error on Your Credit Report
item.description: A clear, step-by-step educational guide to disputing credit report errors with the bureaus under the FCRA and protecting your consumer rights.

item.title: How Long Negative Marks Stay on Your Credit Report
item.description: Understand the general timelines for how long late payments, collections, and bankruptcy can remain on your credit report under federal law.

item.title: The Difference Between FICO and VantageScore
item.description: Compare FICO and VantageScore credit scoring models, how they differ, and why your scores can vary depending on which model a lender uses.

item.title: How to Build Credit From Scratch Responsibly
item.description: A practical, educational guide to building credit from scratch using secured cards, credit-builder loans, and responsible utilization habits.

item.title: How Debt Settlement Actually Works Step By Step
item.description: Learn how debt settlement works step by step, from enrollment and escrow funding to creditor negotiation and the realistic outcomes consumers can expect.

item.title: Understanding Fees In Debt Relief Programs
item.description: Understand how debt-relief program fees are structured, what the FTC advance-fee rule means, and how to compare costs before enrolling in any program.

item.title: How Long Does A Debt Relief Program Take
item.description: Explore typical timelines for debt-relief programs, the factors that lengthen or shorten them, and why no provider can promise a fixed completion date.

item.title: Tax Implications Of Forgiven Debt Explained
item.description: Learn how forgiven debt can become taxable income, what a Form 1099-C means, and why consulting a tax professional about your situation matters.

item.title: How Creditors Decide Whether To Settle
item.description: Understand the incentives, charge-off dynamics, and risk calculations creditors weigh when deciding whether to settle an unsecured debt for less.

item.title: Practical Strategies for Paying Off Medical Debt
item.description: Learn how to review itemized bills, seek financial assistance, and understand medical-debt credit-report rules with this educational overview.

item.title: Managing Student Loan Debt Alongside Credit Cards
item.description: Discover how to prioritize payments and weigh federal student loan options against high-interest credit card debt in this educational guide.

item.title: What to Know About Payday Loan Debt
item.description: Understand the high cost of payday loans, the rollover trap, and lower-cost alternatives in this educational consumer guide.

item.title: Handling Debt After a Job Loss
item.description: Learn how to triage bills, seek hardship programs, and protect essentials after losing a job in this educational guide.
